text.title: mq-broker-implementations Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mq-broker-implementations as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Opening a new pika BlockingConnection per published message inside a request handler.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The RabbitMQClient above opens the connection lazily on every channel access. Under load this thrashes TCP connections. Hold one connection per process and reconnect only on disconnect.

failure-mode.description: Not using long polling (WaitTimeSeconds=0) on SQS receive_message calls.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Short polling returns immediately even when the queue is empty, producing thousands of empty API calls per hour. Long polling (WaitTimeSeconds up to 20) blocks until a message arrives or the timeout expires, dramatically reducing API cost and CPU burn.
